name: Baked Fish with Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Potatoes; peeled and very thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the fish inside and out with salt and pepper to taste, along with the juice of half a lemon.
- If using fillets, sprinkle both sides with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
- Place fennel leaves or seeds inside the fish or sprinkle over the fillets.
2. Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat until it begins to brown.
3. Brown the fish on both sides for about 1-2 minutes per side, being careful not to overcook.
4. Transfer the fish and juices into a 13 x 9-inch baking dish.
5. Arrange thinly sliced onion and potato around the fish.
6. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 30 minutes.
7. Add halved tomatoes on top of the fish, pour white wine over the fish, and sprinkle with Pernod.
8. Continue baking for an additional 10 minutes or until the fish flakes easily with a fork and the potatoes are tender.
9. Serve directly from the baking dish or arrange the fish on a large platter, garnishing with tomatoes, potatoes, and onions.
- Pour any remaining juices over the fish and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
- Garnish with additional lemon halves for serving.
